content:
# Transient Electromagnetic Surveying

**Summary**: An active EM method used for high-resolution mapping of shallow subsurface resistivity.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] utilized transient electromagnetic (TEM) data to produce quasi-2D and 3D depth slices.

- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] employed transient electromagnetic (TEM) soundings to provide high-resolution results for shallower parts of the area.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] used TEM to characterize the geoelectric properties and lithology of the Minjur Aquifer.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] employed transient electromagnetic (TEM) soundings to provide high-resolution results for shallower parts of the area.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] used TEM to characterize the geoelectric properties and lithology of the Minjur Aquifer.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The comparison between MT and TEM data showed agreement in identifying anomalies at the 170-220m depth range. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 77]])

- Used to verify the presence of thermal waters and fractured zones acting as pathways. ([[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024, p. 43]])

- The dual-moment setup allowed for simultaneous detection of shallow and deep strata, improving the depth of investigation. ([[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025, p. 64]])
